## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply. The auto-labeler will handle most labels; tick here for clarity. -->

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `test` — adding or correcting tests
- [ ] `chore` — tooling, deps, CI, housekeeping
- [ ] `breaking-change` — incompatible API or behaviour change (**also add the label**)

## What this changes

<!-- One-paragraph summary. What does the user see/feel that's different? -->

## Why

<!-- The actual motivation. Link issues with `Closes #N`. -->

## How

<!-- Anything non-obvious about the implementation, trade-offs, alternatives considered. -->

## Breaking changes

<!-- If ticked above: describe exactly what breaks and how to migrate. Otherwise delete this section. -->

## Checks

- [ ] `make lint` passes
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] Updated `CHANGELOG.md` under `## [Unreleased]`
- [ ] Updated README/docs if user-facing behaviour changed
